agent_analysis.hypothesis_rationale: Areas with higher nonprofit branch density are denser charity hubs, where organizations are larger and conduct more event-based fundraising, thus spending more on event expenses.
agent_analysis.prior_art_notes: Bielefeld (2000) discusses clustering effects and geographic agglomeration of non-profit operations.
agent_analysis.interpretation: A statistically significant positive correlation was found. An increase of one unit in nonprofit branch density is associated with an average increase of $1,274 in direct event expenses, indicating that local nonprofit density scales alongside the size and event-spending of localized organizations.
